response:
The fare calculation depends on the total distance which includes both driving and converted stopping time. If the distance $\xi$ is less than or equal to 4km then the fare $\eta$ remains fixed at 10 yuan. When the distance $\xi$ exceeds 4km the fare $\eta$ increases by 2 yuan for every extra kilometer rounded up. This means the relationship is $\eta = 10 + 2 \times \lceil \xi - 4 \rceil$ for any distance greater than 4km. The spring season brings many travelers who need to understand these taxi rules clearly. A driver might face various delays that add to the final bill during this busy spring period.

We know the actual fare paid was 38 yuan so we can solve for the total calculated distance. Subtracting the base fare of 10 from 38 leaves 28 yuan which represents the extra distance charges. Dividing 28 by the rate of 2 yuan per kilometer gives us 14 kilometers of excess distance beyond the initial 4km. This means the total calculated distance $\xi$ must have been exactly 18 kilometers for the fare to reach 38 yuan. Since the actual driving distance was 15km the difference of 3km must come from the converted stopping time. Each kilometer of converted time equals 5 minutes so 3 kilometers corresponds to a maximum of 15 minutes of stopping. The taxi could have stopped for at most 15 minutes while traveling between the airport and the hotel.
